YouTube video project, produced by the **{{TEAM_NAME}}** ({{TEAM_ID}}). Provisioned by AgentArchitect; agent/team definitions live in the factory at `{{AA_PATH}}`.

## Routing

Every substantive request in this repo routes through the team orchestrator: invoke `Skill(skill: "{{TEAM_SKILL}}")` and let it run the video pipeline (creative brief → script/storyboard → GATE 1 review → audio → GATE 2 review → visuals → assembly → publish). Do not call specialist agents directly unless the user explicitly names one.

The agents and skills under `.claude/` are **generated** from `{{AA_PATH}}` — never edit them here. To change an agent: edit its source in the factory, then run `aa sync {{TARGET_PATH}}`.
<!-- AA:ROUTING:END -->

## Project layout

```
research/     sources, fact-checks, interview notes
storyboard/   storyboard.md, storyboard-data.json, decks
assets/       images/, audio/narration/, audio/music/, video/ (raw footage)
scripts/      per-project generation/assembly scripts
output/       renders, upload metadata, thumbnails, logs
project.json  creative brief + phase gates status
```

## Production rules (locked preferences)

- **Audio review:** produce BOTH per-scene WAVs (surgical re-record) AND one concatenated MP3 (Gate 2 listening review).
- **Text overlays:** never bake text into images — clean images, animated text overlays in FFmpeg assembly.
- **Vertical video:** pre-crop images to 9:16 before zoompan.
- **Never use DALL-E** — gpt-image-1 or newer only.
- **Before any YouTube upload:** run `{{AA_PATH}}/scripts/youtube-whoami.py` — the token can drift to the wrong channel.
- **Wiki first:** authoritative knowledge base is `~/Workspaces/wiki/`; storyboard reviews are markdown docs in the wiki reviewed in Obsidian.

## Parallel Agent Git Rules (CRITICAL)

- **ONLY commit files YOU changed in THIS session** — always `git add <specific-paths>`, NEVER `git add -A` or `git add .`
- NEVER: `git reset --hard`, `git checkout .` / `git restore .`, `git clean -fd`, `git stash`, `git commit --no-verify`, force-push to main
- Large media (raw footage, renders) stays untracked by default — see `.gitignore`
